skills.name: Programming Fundamentals
skills.description: Core programming skills required for building, automating, and maintaining machine learning systems.
skills.notes: Focus on Python as the primary language for ML, along with Bash for scripting and optionally Go for performance tools. Learn data structures, APIs, and automation scripting.

skills.name: Version Control Systems
skills.description: Tools for tracking code changes and collaborating on machine learning projects.
skills.notes: Git and GitHub are essential for managing code, experiment tracking, and collaboration. Learn branching, merging, and maintaining reproducible workflows.

skills.name: Cloud Computing
skills.description: Using cloud platforms to build, train, and deploy machine learning models at scale.
skills.notes: Covers AWS, Azure, and GCP along with their ML services. Learn compute instances, storage, networking, and managed ML services like SageMaker or Vertex AI.

skills.name: Containerization & Orchestration
skills.description: Packaging ML applications into containers and managing them across distributed systems.
skills.notes: Includes Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for orchestration. Ensures reproducibility and scalability of ML workloads.

skills.name: Machine Learning Fundamentals
skills.description: Understanding core ML concepts, algorithms, and workflows required before operationalizing models.
skills.notes: Covers supervised/unsupervised learning, model training, evaluation, and basic frameworks like scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.

skills.name: Data Engineering Fundamentals
skills.description: Building pipelines and systems to collect, process, and prepare data for machine learning.
skills.notes: Includes data pipelines, ETL processes, data lakes/warehouses, and tools like Spark, Kafka, and Airflow for scalable data workflows.

skills.name: MLOps Practices & Infrastructure
skills.description: Practices and tools for deploying, monitoring, and maintaining ML models in production.
skills.notes: Covers CI/CD for ML, experiment tracking, model registry, feature stores, monitoring, observability, and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, etc.).
